sub_problems: What is the right compact format for one-to-many LLM agent reports — a small fixed parameter vector, a structured token pattern, or a discrete code-book index — that minimizes decoding cost on the receiver side?
How is the encoding learned by the sender agent (instruction-tuning, RL on multi-agent reward, structured-output constraint) and decoded by receivers reliably?
What is the trade-off between expressiveness and decoding-cheapness, and how is the dimension of the signature chosen?
How does the system handle reports about novel content not anticipated by the fixed signature format, and what is the fallback?
